methods.title: GDP per capita
methods.equation: GDP per capita = gross domestic product / population
assumptions: GDP and population use the same economy and reference year.

methods.title: Real GDP growth rate
methods.equation: Growth = real GDP current / real GDP previous − 1
assumptions: Both GDP values use constant prices and a common accounting boundary.

methods.title: Real GDP from nominal GDP and deflator
methods.equation: Real GDP = nominal GDP / (GDP deflator / 100)
assumptions: Deflator and nominal GDP refer to the same year and economy.

methods.title: GDP expenditure identity
methods.equation: GDP = consumption + investment + government + exports − imports
assumptions: Components use compatible national-account definitions and prices.

methods.title: Gross national savings rate
methods.equation: Savings rate = gross savings / GDP
assumptions: Savings and GDP use the same accounting period and price basis.

methods.title: Gross capital formation rate
methods.equation: Investment rate = gross capital formation / GDP
assumptions: Investment and GDP use the same accounting period and price basis.

methods.title: Current-account balance as a share of GDP
methods.equation: Current account = balance / GDP
assumptions: Balance and GDP are measured in the same currency and period.

methods.title: General government fiscal balance
methods.equation: Fiscal balance = revenue − expenditure
assumptions: Government accounts and GDP use the same institutional coverage and period.

methods.title: Labor productivity
methods.equation: Productivity = real output / hours worked
assumptions: Output and hours refer to the same production boundary.

methods.title: Cobb–Douglas total factor productivity
methods.equation: TFP = output / (capital^α × labor^(1−α))
assumptions: Cobb–Douglas production technology and measured factor services.

methods.title: Capital deepening
methods.equation: Capital deepening = capital stock / employment
assumptions: Capital stock and employment refer to the same productive economy.

methods.title: Incremental capital-output ratio
methods.equation: ICOR = investment share / real growth rate
assumptions: Screening estimate using contemporaneous investment and growth.

methods.title: Solow steady-state capital per effective worker
assumptions: Standard Solow model with Cobb–Douglas production and constant rates.

methods.title: Growth-accounting residual
methods.equation: TFP growth = output growth − α capital growth − (1−α) labor growth
assumptions: Growth rates are measured consistently and factor shares sum to one.

methods.title: Income convergence gap
methods.equation: Gap = benchmark income per capita − observed income per capita
assumptions: Benchmark and observed income use comparable methods.

methods.title: Productivity catch-up rate
methods.equation: Catch-up = (gap start − gap end) / gap start
assumptions: Productivity gaps use a common benchmark and index construction.

methods.title: Trade openness
methods.equation: Openness = (exports + imports) / GDP
assumptions: Trade flows and GDP use the same currency and period.

methods.title: Export concentration index
methods.equation: HHI = Σ export-share²
assumptions: Shares are exhaustive and mutually exclusive.

methods.title: Revealed comparative advantage
methods.equation: RCA = country sector share / world sector share
assumptions: Country and world trade data use compatible sector classifications.

methods.title: Terms-of-trade index
methods.equation: Terms of trade = export price index / import price index × 100
assumptions: Export and import price indices use the same base period.

methods.title: Real exchange-rate index
methods.equation: RER = nominal exchange rate × foreign price / domestic price
assumptions: Price indices use compatible baskets and the exchange-rate convention is explicit.

methods.title: Employment shift from agriculture
methods.equation: Shift = agriculture employment share start − end
assumptions: Employment shares use a common labor-force definition.

methods.title: Manufacturing value-added share
methods.equation: Share = manufacturing value added / GDP
assumptions: Manufacturing value added and GDP use compatible prices and classifications.

methods.title: Structural transformation index
methods.equation: Index = 0.5 × Σ|sector share end − start|
assumptions: Sector shares exhaust the economy in each period.

methods.title: Employment-to-population rate
methods.equation: Employment rate = employed / working-age population
assumptions: Employment and working-age population use consistent definitions.

methods.title: Unemployment rate
methods.equation: Unemployment rate = unemployed / labor force
assumptions: Unemployment and labor force use the same survey definition.

methods.title: Labor-force participation rate
methods.equation: Participation = labor force / working-age population
assumptions: Labor force and working-age population use a common age range.

methods.title: Labor income share
methods.equation: Labor share = employee compensation / gross value added
assumptions: Compensation and value added use the same production boundary.

methods.title: Three-group Gini approximation
methods.equation: Gini = 1 − Σ[(Y_i + Y_i−1)(X_i − X_i−1)]
assumptions: Groups are ordered from lowest to highest income and shares sum to one.

methods.title: Palma ratio
methods.equation: Palma ratio = top 10% income share / bottom 40% income share
assumptions: Income shares use the same welfare concept and household weighting.

methods.title: Poverty gap index
methods.equation: Poverty gap = poor population × normalized income shortfall / total population
assumptions: Average poor income and poverty line use the same period and price basis.

methods.title: Gender wage ratio
methods.equation: Wage ratio = comparison-group wage / reference-group wage
assumptions: Wages are adjusted for the same period and compensation concept.

methods.title: Human development geometric mean
methods.equation: HDI = (health × education × income)^(1/3)
assumptions: Component indices are normalized consistently.

methods.title: Upper-secondary attainment rate
methods.equation: Attainment = credentialed population / relevant population
assumptions: Credential and population definitions are consistent.

methods.title: Life-expectancy index
methods.equation: Index = (life expectancy − minimum) / (maximum − minimum)
assumptions: Goalposts are explicit scenario or reporting choices.

methods.title: Health spending per capita
methods.equation: Spending per capita = health expenditure / population
assumptions: Health expenditure and population use the same jurisdiction and year.

methods.title: Tax-to-GDP ratio
methods.equation: Tax ratio = tax revenue / GDP
assumptions: Tax revenue and GDP use a common period and institutional boundary.

methods.title: Public debt-to-GDP ratio
methods.equation: Debt ratio = gross public debt / GDP
assumptions: Debt and GDP use the same currency and reporting-date convention.

methods.title: External debt-service burden
methods.equation: Debt service burden = debt service / export earnings
assumptions: Debt service and export earnings use the same period and currency.

methods.title: Social protection coverage
methods.equation: Coverage = protected population / target population
assumptions: Protected and target populations use a common program definition.

methods.title: Official development assistance per capita
methods.equation: ODA per capita = net ODA / population
assumptions: ODA and population refer to the same recipient geography and year.

methods.title: Foreign direct investment inflows as a share of GDP
methods.equation: FDI share = net FDI inflows / GDP
assumptions: FDI and GDP use compatible currency and period definitions.

methods.title: Personal remittances as a share of GDP
methods.equation: Remittance share = remittance receipts / GDP
assumptions: Remittances and GDP use compatible currency and period definitions.

methods.title: Infrastructure financing gap
methods.equation: Gap = required investment − available financing
assumptions: Requirement and available financing use the same investment period and scope.

methods.title: Sustainable development financing gap
methods.equation: Gap = annual SDG financing need − committed finance
assumptions: Need and committed finance use the same SDG scope and year.

methods.title: Inclusive growth score
methods.equation: Score = weighted growth + employment + poverty reduction + equality
assumptions: Scores use a common scale and weights are explicit governance choices.

methods.title: Resilience-adjusted development score
methods.equation: Adjusted score = development score × resilience factor
assumptions: Development score and resilience factor are transparently documented.

methods.title: Development scenario robustness
methods.equation: Robustness = minimum scenario performance / target performance
assumptions: Scenario scores use the same indicator framework and target.
